sections.category: LLM Workflow > OpenClaw / NemoClaw / Hermes Agents

bullets.body: **OpenClaw 2026.4.14** (Apr 14) — Reliability gains in plugin loading, memory / dreaming. Expanded local-model choices. Better Feishu config flow. Claude Opus 4.7 as default. Gemini TTS (bundled Google plugin). Model auth status card. Cloud-backed LanceDB memory. Copilot embedding. Plugin packaging slimmed down. Fixes across agent / UI / gateway / messaging. Community reviews continue across the 3-day window.

**Hermes Agent (Nous Research) on a fast cadence** (7 majors from late March through mid-April). Autonomous skill generation, cross-session memory, integrations with Telegram / Discord / Slack / WhatsApp / Signal / email. Reviews verified as of 2026-04-17.

citations.label: DEV Community - Hermes Agent Review 95.6K Stars

bullets.body: **NemoClaw (NVIDIA)** — Continues from the March preview. The enterprise stack that wraps OpenClaw with audit logging / access control / guardrails. No new announcement in the window, but pairing with OpenClaw 2026.4.14 is the focal point.

**v0.19.1 (patch)** — Applies Transformers v5.5.4 upgrade and a Gemma4 bug fix on top of v0.19.0. Inherits the v0.19.0 capability set: full Gemma 4 (MoE / multimodal / reasoning / tool use), Zero-Bubble Async Scheduling + speculative decoding, mature Model Runner V2, full ViT CUDA-graph support, and Transformers v5 compatibility.

**v0.5.10.post1 (Apr 8) is the latest stable** — No new release confirmed inside the 3-day window. Recent flagship features remain piecewise CUDA Graph defaults, Elastic EP (DeepSeek MoE partial-failure resilience), and HiSparse sparse attention.
**Claude Opus 4.7 expands to 27 AWS regions.** New-generation inference engine with dynamic capacity allocation and zero operator access (vendor staff cannot view customer data).

citations.label: AWS News Blog - Claude Opus 4.7 in Amazon Bedrock

bullets.body: **Enterprise Security for Claude Code webinar** announced for 2026-04-21 11:00 PST.

citations.label: AWS - Claude Opus 4.7 now available in Amazon Bedrock

bullets.body: **Qwen3.6-35B-A3B (open weights, released Apr 16)** — 262K context, SWE-bench Verified 73.4%, GPQA 86.0%, AIME 2026 92.7%. Simon Willison reports on Apr 16 that local inference on his laptop "drew me a better pelican than Claude Opus 4.7." Discussion continues through the window.

citations.label: Simon Willison - Qwen3.6-35B-A3B drew me a better pelican than Claude Opus 4.7

bullets.body: **Qwen3.6-Plus (proprietary)** — SWE-bench Verified 78.8%, Terminal-Bench 2.0 61.6%, 1M context. BenchLM composite 77/100, 23rd of 109 models.
**Bonsai-1.7B on CUDA deployment guide** (Dev|Journal, 2026-04-19) — Hands-on coverage of GGUF benchmarks, chat, JSON structured output, and RAG. RTX 4090 hits 674 tok/s (vs 224 tok/s at FP16 — 3x).

citations.label: Dev|Journal - Deploying PrismML Bonsai-1.7B on CUDA

bullets.body: **Bonsai local-execution tutorial** (2026-04-17) — Focused on home-lab / personal-device deployment.

citations.label: Braincuber - Run Bonsai 1-Bit LLM Locally (2026 Guide)

bullets.body: **Bankai (XOR binary-weight patch)** — First post-hoc adaptation method for 1-bit LLMs. Sparse XOR bitmask rewrites weights in place; patches compress to ~1 KB.

**b8838 (2026-04-18)** — `llama-cli --endpoint` for remote-server connection, prefix-cache fix for the Anthropic API, request-handling improvements for WebUI thinking mode, and a verifiable-inference sample (commit-and-open via SHA-256 / Merkle trees).

**v0.20.0** — Day-1 Gemma 4 support (released Apr 2, 24-hour turnaround). MLX-based Apple Silicon support. Restored Gemma 4 nothink renderer. Fixed compiler errors in the Gemma 4 Metal build. No new store release inside the window, but native Qwen3.6 support reports keep growing.

sections.category: Ecosystems (local inference runtimes) > Azure Foundry Local

bullets.body: No new announcement inside the window. Comparison articles continue to position Foundry Local as the enterprise on-device inference choice.

citations.label: Knowledge Share - Mastering Azure Foundry Local

bullets.body: **Indirect prompt injection PoC over GitHub Actions** — Anthropic Claude Code Security Review, Google Gemini CLI Action, and GitHub Copilot Agent are all vulnerable. A prompt embedded in PR title / comment / Issue body exfiltrates GITHUB_TOKEN or env vars. Found by Aonan Guan + Johns Hopkins researchers. All three vendors silently patched without CVE issuance (bounties: Anthropic $100, GitHub $500, Google undisclosed).

citations.label: SecurityWeek - Claude Code, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot Agents Vulnerable to Prompt Injection via Comments

bullets.body: **Pushpaganda campaign** — HUMAN Satori reports the campaign spreading on Hacker News. SEO poisoning + AI-generated content corrupts Google Discover feeds; browser notifications then push scareware / ad fraud. Targets Android / Chrome users.

citations.label: The Hacker News - AI-Driven Pushpaganda Scam

bullets.body: **OpenAI's official position** — "Prompt injection is like social engineering and cannot be fully solved." Mitigation, not elimination, is the industry's working consensus.
**CVE-2026-33824** — Windows IKE Service Extensions double free. Unauthenticated RCE. CVSS **9.8**.
**CVE-2026-33827** — Windows TCP/IP race condition. Unauthenticated RCE. CVSS **8.1** (CrowdStrike) / **9.8** (Windows News) — scoring varies.

citations.label: Windows News - CVE-2026-33827 Windows TCP/IP RCE

bullets.body: **CVE-2026-33826** — Windows AD improper input validation (CWE-20). Authenticated attacker → RCE via RPC. CVSS **8.0**.

citations.label: Tenable - Microsoft's April 2026 Patch Tuesday Addresses 163 CVEs

bullets.body: **CVE-2026-32157** — Remote Desktop Client use-after-free. Unauthenticated RCE. CVSS **8.8**.
**CVE-2026-35616** — Fortinet FortiClient EMS, reported under active exploitation. High urgency in the window.

citations.label: The Hacker News - Fortinet Patches Actively Exploited CVE-2026-35616

bullets.body: **CVE-2026-20180 / CVE-2026-20186** — Cisco ISE, privilege escalation via insufficient input validation.
**CVE-2026-27876 / CVE-2026-27880** — Grafana Critical / High.

citations.label: Grafana - Security Release CVE-2026-27876 and CVE-2026-27880

sections.category: Bay Area / Silicon Valley AI Meetups

bullets.body: **AI Tinkerers San Francisco** (monthly, live coding demos, no slides — late-April session announced).
**Bond AI - SF and Bay Area** (Luma calendar, events inside the window).
**SF AI Meetup** (sfbay-ai / SF AI) — Recurring talks on GenAI / LLM / Agent practice.
**AgentCon San Francisco** (2026-05-04 announced) — A developer conference focused on AI agents.
**MIT Technology Review "10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now"** scheduled for 2026-04-21 (not a Bay Area event but heavily covered in Bay Area media).
**Simon Willison (2026-04-19)** — "Headless everything for personal AI." Argues that personal AI is faster and more reliable hitting headless services directly than driving a GUI with mouse moves.

citations.label: Simon Willison - Headless everything for personal AI

bullets.body: **Simon Willison (2026-04-17)** — Datasette 1.0a28 release; PyCon US 2026 AI / Security track announcement.
**Cadence × NVIDIA expanded partnership** — Announced at CadenceLIVE Silicon Valley 2026. Multi-physics simulation integrated with Isaac Robotics / Cosmos world models.
**Anthropic's annualized revenue tops $30B** — Reportedly above OpenAI's $25B.

**Iowa State University research (2026-04-19)** — Argues that anthropomorphic language about AI systems is being abused in media coverage. [ScienceDaily - Think AI knows what it's doing? Scientists say think again](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/04/260417224505.htm) ---

citations.label: ScienceDaily - Think AI knows what it's doing? Scientists say think again
